response.justification: Candidate has 13+ years of software development experience and is technically strong, but is fundamentally a web/full-stack engineer (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) rather than a C++ developer. His only C++ role was a ~6-month stint at National Instruments over 10 years ago. The role's core requirements — modern C++/STL expertise, industrial cybersecurity (IEC 62443), MQTT, message brokers in C++, and industrial automation domain — are essentially absent from the resume. Years of experience match, but the technical stack alignment is very weak.
strengths: 13+ years of software engineering experience with strong fundamentals (TDD, CI/CD, architecture)
Some low-latency, multi-threaded, async I/O exposure via DLang microservices at Sociomantic
Mentoring and technical communication track record (workshops, courses, talks)
concerns: Minimal and outdated C++ experience (~6 months at NI in 2014–2015); primary stack is TypeScript/Node/DLang
No demonstrated experience with IEC 62443, SDL, MQTT, or industrial-automation protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, etc.)
No industrial automation or IoT domain background; career is web, mobile, and GIS-focused

key_factors.factor: Years of experience and engineering maturity
